title: Rochelia patula
taxonomy: Rochelia patula is classified in the family Boraginaceae. This family of plants is commonly known as the borage or forget-me-not family. The genus Rochelia is a group of flowering plants that are native to the Mediterranean region. Rochelia patula, in particular, is known for its showy, blue flowers and is often grown as an ornamental plant. As a member of the Boraginaceae family, Rochelia patula shares characteristics with other plants in this group.

intro: Rochelia patula, also known as Patula or Patula plant, is a flowering plant belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae) and is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Rochelia patula include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Rochelia patula is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
